titleEn: Second screwworm case confirmed miles from first Texas detection
titleZh: 得克萨斯州确认第二例螺旋蝇病例，距首例仅数英里
summaryZh: 美国农业部在得克萨斯州确认了第二例食肉螺旋蝇寄生虫病例，距首例仅5.6英里。感染发生在扎瓦拉县一头一个月大的小牛身上。该疫情对全美最大养牛州得克萨斯构成重大经济威胁，美国已关闭与墨西哥的活牛进口边境一年多，并投入数百万美元防控。
featuredReason: 该新闻直接关系到美国最大养牛州的重大动物疫情，对智慧畜牧行业而言，疫情监测、预警系统和生物安全防控技术的需求将显著提升，值得关注相关技术应用机会。
contentFull: Health & Disease Notfiable Diseases New World Screwworm Second screwworm case confirmed miles from first Texas detection New infection found in one-month-old calf in same county 8 June 2026 1 minute read By: Global Ag Media North America A second case of the flesh-eating screwworm parasite was confirmed in Texas by the US Department of Agriculture on Friday, emerging just miles from where the first US detection in decades was reported this week, reported Reuters. The new case in Zavala County was detected on a ranch 5.6 miles (9 km) from the first positive case of screwworm in Texas, which the USDA confirmed on Wednesday.The infection, which the USDA said was in a one-month-old calf, was reported earlier on Friday by Reuters, citing sources.The USDA discovered the second infestation "after testing a number of suspect cases," the department's Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service said in a press release.APHIS and Texas Animal Health Commission officials are continuing to "collect and test other samples from the surrounding area which have come back negative," it said.Friday's case and the initial detection in nearby La Pryor, a town roughly 30 miles (50 km) northeast of the US-Mexico border, have dealt a setback to US cattle ranchers, who have been preparing for the arrival of the pest as it has moved north through Mexico over the past year.Screwworms are parasitic flies that deposit eggs in open wounds or mucous membranes of warm-blooded animals. After hatching, the larvae penetrate living tissue, feeding on the host and potentially causing fatal damage if not treated.An outbreak in US border states in the 1960s devastated wildlife and inflicted heavy financial losses on ranchers.A widespread resurgence now could pose a significant economic threat in Texas, the country’s largest cattle-producing state, through animal deaths as well as higher labour and treatment costs.To limit the risk, Washington has kept the US-Mexico border closed to live cattle imports for more than a year and has spent millions of dollars to curb the pest’s northward spread, including funding sterile fly production, expanding trapping programs and stepping up livestock monitoring. Our Partners C-Lock dsm-firmenich Zoetis FAI Heifer International Hamlet Protein Global Ag Media More from this author More News View all FAO joins Canadian ranchers to mark International Year of Rangelands 17 July 2026 1 minute read Cattle futures hit lowest since March - CME Markets Market Trends Economics 16 July 2026 1 minute read Hog futures climb as pork cutout tops $101 US to impose 25% tariff on most Brazilian imports from July 22 Markets Policy Trade 16 July 2026 2 minute read Beef, coffee and aircraft exempted as Lula vows WTO challenge Our Partners C-Lock dsm-firmenich Zoetis FAI Heifer International Hamlet Protein
translationZh:
美国农业部周五在得克萨斯州确认了第二例食肉螺旋蝇寄生虫病例，该病例出现在本周报告的数十年美国首例病例仅数英里的地方，据路透社报道。

扎瓦拉县的新病例是在距得克萨斯州首例阳性螺旋蝇病例5.6英里（9公里）的一个牧场发现的，美国农业部周三确认了首例病例。

美国农业部表示，感染发生在一头一个月大的小牛身上，路透社周五早些时候援引消息来源报道了这一情况。

美国农业部动植物卫生检验局在一份新闻稿中表示，在“检测了多个疑似病例后”发现了第二次感染。

APHIS和得克萨斯州动物卫生委员会的官员正在继续“从周边地区收集并检测其他样本，这些样本结果均为阴性”，该机构表示。

周五的病例以及最初在拉普赖尔（一个距美墨边境约30英里/50公里的小镇）附近的检测，对美国养牛户造成了打击，他们过去一年一直在为这种害虫从墨西哥向北移动做准备。

螺旋蝇是一种寄生蝇，会将卵产在温血动物的开放性伤口或黏膜中。孵化后，幼虫会穿透活体组织，以宿主为食，如果不加治疗，可能导致致命损伤。

20世纪60年代美国边境各州的疫情曾对野生动物造成毁灭性打击，并给牧场主带来巨额经济损失。

现在，如果疫情广泛复发，可能对得克萨斯州（美国最大的养牛州）构成重大经济威胁，包括动物死亡以及更高的劳动力和治疗成本。

为降低风险，华盛顿已关闭美墨边境的活牛进口超过一年，并投入数百万美元遏制该害虫向北蔓延，包括资助不育蝇生产、扩大诱捕计划以及加强牲畜监测。
contentHtml:
Second screwworm case confirmed miles from first Texas detection 
New infection found in one-month-old calf in same county
